URL: https://www.irishtimes.com/news/ireland/irish-news/two-men-arrested-after-drug-seizure-worth-180-000-in-cork-1.3693614
COUNTRY: ie
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Two men arrested after drug seizure worth _180000 in Cork

CONTENT: Two men arrested after drug seizure worth ? 180,000 in Cork It is understood that the seizure includes 10 kilogrammes of cannabis resin with an street value of ? 60,000 Garda ? ? in Cork are to prepare a file for the DPP after releasing without charge two men arrested for questioning about the discovery of ? 180,000 worth of drugs in Passage West . Detectives from the Cork City Divisional Drugs Unit searched two houses in the Maulbaun Estate in Passage West at 11pm on Friday as part of a planned operation against the sale of drugs in Cork . They recovered quantities of cannabis resin , ecstasy tablets , cannabis herb and cocaine along with some cash in the searches and the drugs have been sent to the state laboratory for analysis . It is understood that the seizure includes 10 kilos of cannabis resin with an street value of ? 60,000 , a kilo of cannabis herb worth ? 20,000 and a quarter kilo of cocaine worth approximately ? 20,000 . It is also understood that the balance of the seizure includes several thousand ecstasy tablets estimated to be @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ in cash found in one of the houses . Two men , one an English national in his 50s and the second , an Irish national in his late 40s , were arrested . They were taken to the Bridewell Garda station in Cork city where they were questioned by detectives for several hours about the seizure which gardai described as " significant " . The men were released without charge late on Saturday evening and gardai will prepare a file on the matter for the DPP , a Garda spokesman said .

URL: https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/news/greater-manchester-news/gps-nhs-strike-patients-wages-24375677
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Why GPs like me could be about to go on strike

CONTENT: Sign up to the MyTameside newsletter here to keep abreast of what 's happening in your neighbourhood Something went wrong , please try again later.Invalid Email We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you 've consented to and to improve our understanding of you . This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding . You can unsubscribe at any time . More info " It 's busy . Normally , we get a quieter period in June and July , but it 's just been relentless , " says one Greater Manchester GP . Dr Faisal Bhutta has been ' working all of the time ' for months now , as patients come forward with problems they have kept to themselves during the pandemic , he says . And instead of demand returning to some sense of normalcy , NHS GPs are now facing a new contract that would see them offer yet more appointments in the evenings and on weekends to meet the sheer volume of people needing help . This week , medics across the country have come out in force to protest the @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Dr Bhutta says that there is actually another important reason why GPs could strike - pay . GPs have been under a ' pay freeze ' for around five years , he claims , limiting doctors to a one per cent rise each year . Practices have prioritised giving pay rises to other clinical and non-clinical staff , says Dr Bhutta , but by the time doctors ' wages are considered , there is no funding left in the pots of practices to hand out the raises . In 2021 , there was outcry from GPs after the government offered a one per cent pay increase for doctors . This was then upped to three per cent after the Review Body on Doctors ' and Dentists ' Remuneration . But at the time , GP committee chair Dr Richard Vautrey said the pay rise was a ' completely empty promise ' . He said the government had not given ' partners the funding needed to pay the 3 per cent uplift ' . " It 's becoming a challenge to find people who want to come @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ financially sustainable and the workload is a real drawback , " says Dr Bhutta ( Image : ABNM Photography ) A year on , Dr Bhutta says partners at GP practices are still facing the same cashflow problems . Meanwhile , days at work are only getting harder . " People have been keeping to themselves and not coming forward with their illnesses or problems . Everyone is coming out with things they have sat on , everybody is coming to see us , " he told the Manchester Evening News . " As GPs , our pay has been frozen for four to five years , limited to around one per cent increase . But all the while , the workload is going up , we 're asked to do more and more , including possibly starting seven day working - though I would n't say that 's the main reason for GPs wanting to strike . " The main reason is the massive increase in workload , working conditions and the pay increase freeze . We 're paying our staff at the practice extra @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ our pay has n't gone up for the last five years . " As well as working as a GP , Dr Bhutta is a long Covid specialist in Greater Manchester ( Image : ABNM Photography ) In March , NHS England announced changes to the 2022/23 GP contract , saying doctors needed to make at least 25 per cent of appointments available for online booking while also extending opening hours . The changes would mean GPs need to offer appointments from 9am to 5pm on Saturdays , as well as until 8pm on weekdays . The British Medical Association ( BMA ) slammed the contract , saying NHS England had refused to offer practices reimbursement to cover additional costs for national insurance contributions , which could lead to cuts in staffing . Dr Bhutta says he , too , fears that lack of funding will lead to layoffs . Read More Related Articles Medics at the BMA annual conference in Brighton called on BMA leaders to act upon a 2021 indicative ballot and " organise opposition " to the contract , " including @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Health and Social Care spokesperson said : " We hugely value all our general practice staff and are incredibly grateful for their tireless efforts to provide care for us all . Evening and weekend GP appointments have been available to patients for several years , and the new contract aims to remove the current variation across the country and provide a more consistent offer for patients to level up health disparities . " Primary Care Networks were agreed with the BMA in 2019 and have provided millions of pounds of additional funding for primary care , including reimbursement for over 18,000 primary care professionals . " This Tameside GP says getting round the negotiating table should be the resolution to the pay question , otherwise , patients will suffer . But how to fix a dwindling number of medics wanting to go into general practice , and save the profession long-term , is another matter entirely . Dr Bhutta 's surgery in Denton ( Image : ABNM Photography ) " Even so , I do n't think striking is the right thing to do , I @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ explains Dr Bhutta . " Striking would affect the public and our patients too much , it 's not their fault and they would suffer . " It 's a difficult job , I 'm not sure what we can do to attract more people to general practice . It 's becoming more and more unsustainable . " It 's becoming a challenge to find people who want to come to general practices and become partners . It 's not financially sustainable and the workload is a real drawback " Read More Related Articles If a plan does not emerge ' the quality of care ' will be affected , says the Tameside doctor . There 's currently not enough GPs to fill that new contract , even if they wanted to , he admits . " There 's a limit to what you can do given the resources . We ca n't keep providing that level of work which should be being done by more people , it will affect the quality of care and the number of appointments we do . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ days a week by utilising staff who are n't doctors because there are more health care professionals and nurses available . But I do n't think we have enough doctors to meet that kind of working pattern . " We 're all struggling in the hours we already do , you could n't find GPs willing to do it even if you did give them more money . We 're all already working all of the time . "

TITLE: An Irish woman is making rugby history in the European Challenge ...

CONTENT: The English side ... Bristol in the Challenge Cup at the Recreation Ground , and although people might not pay much attention to the identity of the touch judge , it is pretty significant . Neville , who won the Grand Slam with the Irish Women 's team in 2013 , has since become a referee and has quickly risen up through the ranks . She has previously ran the line in the Pro12 but tonight she was given her European debut , making history as the first woman to ever serve as a touch judge in either the Champions Cup or the Challenge Cup . Hopefully this is just the beginning for Neville and we see her taking charge of a big game in the near future . TITLE: Jewellery Quarter traders concerned over workshops up for sale

CONTENT: Image caption Traders said they fear they will be made to leave by future owners Jewellery repair shop owner William Rose said : " I am very worried about it . We have all been here for quite a number of years . " They are driving us out to turn them into apartments . Where do we go ? " Mould cutter Steven Peyzer said : " Preferably we would like to stay where we are . " We ca n't stop the council from selling their properties , but we would like an insurance we can stay where we are . " The Jewellery Quarter sites are part of nine properties across Birmingham being offered for informal tender by the council . A Birmingham City Council spokesman said : " Vyse Street Triangle is one of nine sites being offered for sale by informal tender , having been identified as having the potential to support housing growth and job creation in Birmingham . " This particular site is being sold as a leasehold and this includes existing tenancies , protected under the Landlord and Tenant Act for the duration @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ advice being offered to prospective bidders said new owners should look to " refurbish and convert the existing buildings and retain some or all of the commercial use " .

URL: https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2023/feb/20/endangered-african-penguin-chicks-arizona-aquarium
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Scottsdale aquarium welcomes endangered African penguin chicks

CONTENT: Arizona aquarium welcomes endangered African penguin chicks An aquarium in Arizona has welcomed three new arrivals in its population of endangered African penguins . In an Instagram post , the OdySea Aquarium in Scottsdale showed the fluffy chicks being gently held by staff , as they were yet to fully stand up on their own . " These tiny arrivals are extremely valuable genetically to the endangered African penguin species , as they were born to parents , Mojo and Lemieux , Bubbles and Weasley , whose pairings are considered highly valuable within the zoological community , " the aquarium said in the post . " The successful hatching of these three chicks , supports the goal of building healthy genetic populations within zoos and aquariums . " Allow Instagram content ? This article includes content provided by Instagram . We ask for your permission before anything is loaded , as they may be using cookies and other technologies . To view this content , click ' Allow and continue ' . Two of the chicks were born to Mojo and Lemieux , penguins previously paired as part of the zoo @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ third chick was born to Bubbles and Weasley , both first-time parents . The three chicks were being referred to as 42 , 43 and 44 while they awaited names . Their sexes were yet to be determined . There are 40 African penguins at the Scottsdale aquarium , which has reported 13 successful hatchings . The population of the only penguin species found in Africa has been experiencing steep decline , numbers decreasing by more than 95% since pre-industrial times . If climate change and human activity patterns continue , experts estimate the species will be extinct in the wild in the next three years . " As the population of the African penguin continues to rapidly decline -- down 23% in the past two years alone -- OdySea Aquarium remains committed to the survival of the species in partnership with other Association of Zoos and Aquariums ' accredited facilities , " Jess Peranteau , the aquarium director of animal care and education , told the AP .

URL: http://www.limerickpost.ie/2017/06/20/chamber-awards-set-to-reflect-limericks-rising-star-status/
COUNTRY: ie
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Chamber awards set to reflect Limerick's rising star status

CONTENT: THE annual Limerick Chamber Business Awards are set to become the biggest the forum has seen in this its 7th year with 14 categories now up for finalisation . Chamber CEO Dr James Ring said that this year 's awards will reflect Limerick 's status as an economic rising star . From the 2016 bill of 12 categories , two new awards have been added for this November 's gala event ; Best Employer and Best Innovation ( RD&I ) Award Speaking ahead of the official launch next Monday morning at the Castletroy Park Hotel , Dr Ring said that the event will capture the unprecedented sense of positivity and confidence in Limerick today . The awards are sponsored by LIT and will recognise and celebrate business success across all sectors and scales , from SME 's right up to multinationals . They are open to all businesses -- not just Chamber members -- in Limerick , Clare , Tipperary , North Cork and North Kerry . " In 2016 the Mid-West was the fastest growing region in terms of employment and it is also the fastest growing region outside the capital in @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ as one national newspaper headline belted out last week , the poster boy of the economic recovery , " said Dr. Ring . " It has happened by virtue of a true collective approach , including from the business community itself . Our awards in November , more than any other event , recognise that effort and celebrate those companies that are standing out in a really vibrant city and region today , " said Dr. Ring . The Chamber CEO urged as many companies as possible to enter the Awards this year , emphasising that the return for winning and even shortlisted companies has gone beyond the award itself . " Companies who have been shortlisted have found the recognition and profile received motivates and encourages them and others to strive for more success . Thanks to our media partners , the Limerick Leader and Limerick 's Live 95FM , every one of the shortlisted companies is profiled , spreading awareness of their operations and , indeed , products and services . " " Being a well-known organisation or having a famous consumer brand name is @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ more challenging if you are a small , yet unknown company with opportunities to grow globally with the right team . Organisations need to actively promote their strengths , and demonstrate why they should be considered a preferred employer to work for , " Dr Ring added . The 13 Categories for the 2017 Awards are : Best Innovative Startup of the Year Best SME Business of the Year Best Large Business of the Year Continue reading below ... Best Contribution to the Community ( Corporate Social Responsibility ) of the Year Best Community & Voluntary Sector Organisation of the Year Best Family Business of the Year Best Retail or Food Service Business of the Year Best Hospitality , Tourism & Culture Business of the Year Best Business Services Provider of the Year Best Innovation ( RD&I ) Award of the Year Best Employer : Talent Development & Workplace Wellness of the Year The Limerick Chamber Regional Business of the Year ( chosen from the Winners of the above Categories ) Limerick Chamber President 's @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ for application The closing date for entries is Monday 24th September 2017 . See www.limerickchamber.ie for more with the awards scheduled for the Limerick Chamber President 's Dinner on Friday November 17 . URL: https://www.theguardian.com/football/2017/dec/09/vincent-kompany-manchester-city-united-shift-in-power
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Vincent Kompany: 'A shift in power? Only if City dominate for another ...

CONTENT: Almost a decade after his arrival marked a new era in English football , Vincent Kompany is in a good position to make a judgment . Having started his career at Anderlecht , the future Manchester City captain was a fresh-faced 22-year-old when he was purchased from Hamburg at the end of August 2008 by Mark Hughes , the first of a total of 78 signings totalling more than ? 1bn that have transformed the club since Sheikh Mansour 's takeover that summer . As a result , with Pep Guardiola 's vibrant team turning heads around Europe as they prepare to face Manchester United in the most significant derby for years , Kompany is by now well-versed in answering the city 's perennial question . " A shift in power ? I 'd like to see us dominate for another 10 years , " he says . As the son of a political refugee from Zaire who became a diplomat , and with a mother who was a trade-union leader in Brussels , Kompany has never been afraid to voice an opinion . He established himself as City 's captain within two seasons of joining @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , two League Cups and an FA Cup , enabling the " noisy neighbours " to overtake United in the years since the departure of Sir Alex Ferguson . But at 31 and with a list of injuries ( 18 ) that exceeds the number of goals he has scored in a sky-blue shirt ( 16 ) , the Belgium defender knows he can take nothing for granted as the final year of his contract approaches . " I have been a part of trying to turn this club around . When I signed for City we were out and about in town a lot because we were n't the big dogs in the city , " he recalls . " That 's changed a bit now . We 're now more like an international club , but it was n't like that when I signed . When the owners came to Manchester City they decided they wanted to make this club a huge club in England . At the same time , there was a huge gap left when Ferguson left United . " It 's been @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ a rebuilding phase , we were in the ascendancy . But one thing I would like to say , touching on Ferguson , is that it is absolutely normal that when a person has had such a big influence on a club for such a long time , there will always be a big rebuilding job to do once he has left . " That 's why I am reluctant to say there has been a shift in power . I 'm not defending United . They have made a huge investment to try to close the gap on us and fill the void left by Ferguson . But it is normal that it would take them a few years to steady the ship once he left . " Eight points separate the two rivals at the top of the table going into Sunday 's meeting at Old Trafford , with City aiming to match Arsenal 's record of 14 successive Premier League victories . Kompany will face a late fitness test after withdrawing from the team that went on to lose to Shakhtar Donetsk in the Champions @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ in the warm-up in Ukraine . Having already missed almost three months of the campaign following yet another calf problem , his desire to be involved in Guardiola 's all-conquering team is clear . " The first thing you feel when you are injured is : ' I want to be a part of it . I want to be a part of this ' . And as soon as the game finishes you 're thinking : ' I need to train , I need to do something , I need to somehow get myself back on track to participate ' , " Kompany says . " I feel like it 's a great sense of motivation you get out of it . What I have learned over the years , and this could help a lot of players when they go through an injury spell , is that you can still improve when you 're injured , even if you 're not on the pitch or lacing up your boots . " Just by visualising and imagining yourself in certain situations and that idea of doing @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ the key as to why I 've always been able to slot right back in even though I 've not been playing for a month , two months . I 've just visualised myself among the lads so many times doing it and if the physical aspect of it is OK you come back and realise you have made improvements . " With John Stones absent until the new year , City 's achilles heel at the back could yet be exposed should their captain face another spell on the sidelines . A United side deprived of the suspended Paul Pogba may not be the best-equipped to take advantage , although Kompany remains wary of the threat posed by his compatriot Romelu Lukaku despite his recent barren run in front of goal . Kompany is also at pains to warn against any complacency should they emerge with the points . This is still City , after all . " We have n't won anything . For all it is worth , we are just a few points ahead of them and that is it . Everything else , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ the results , it wo n't matter until we have some silverware to show for it . So until then , I would n't be worried if I was another club . If we start winning stuff consistently and in two or three years ' time we are having the same discussion , it is a different debate then . But not at the moment . "

TITLE: Amazon reveals Greystones is Ireland's self-publishing capital

CONTENT: Meath , Wicklow ... Kildare have most independently published authors per person Greystones in Co Wicklow has been revealed as Ireland 's publishing hotspot according to new data released by Amazon . The coastal town , which perhaps not coincidentally also has the most expensive average house price outisde Dublin , has more independently published authors per person than any other town in Ireland . The town already has several literary associations . Samuel Beckett had one of his defining epiphanies as a writer , as expressed in Krapp 's Last Tape , on a stormy night on Greystones ' old pier , and his father is buried in the town . Safe Harbour by Marita Conlon-McKenna , about child evacuees during the second World War , is set there . Ruth Fitzmaurice , author of I Found My Tribe , forthcoming next year from Chatto & Wilnus with a film deal already signed , is a resident , as is Dave Lordan , editor of Young Irelanders , the New Island anthology . Paul Howard , Ross O'Carroll-Kelly 's muse , is also a former resident . Joining Greystones in the top five are Mullingar , Enniscorthy , Maynooth @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ using Amazon 's Kindle Direct Publishing ( KDP ) programme . The top 10 towns in Ireland are : The figures are based on the number of KDP authors in towns with a population of over 10,000 people , divided by population . While some towns may be recent additions to the literary scene , others have a rich history in books and publishing . James Joyce lived in Bray during his youth and 2005 Booker Prize-winning author John Banville is from Wexford . Pedro Huerta , director of Kindle Content Europe , said : " KDP allows anyone with the desire to write a book to get their story read locally and around the world . Writing your own book is very popular in Ireland , with scores of authors publishing every day on KDP . We 've seen a real growth in counties like Meath , Wicklow and Kildare over the last few years , and it 's great to see so many writers publish their work for the enjoyment of readers around the world . TITLE: DWP will make seven payments and handouts worth 4,385 before Christmas

CONTENT: Get the latest Birmingham Live breaking news on WhatsApp UK households are due seven Department for Work and Pensions payments before Christmas worth a staggering ? 4,385 . The alert to UK households comes as the DWP looks to help tide people who are hard-up and pressed for cash amid the Cost of Living crisis . Despite the Winter Fuel Payment being scrapped , there are still benefit payments due to land in bank accounts before the festive season . And for some of the handy schemes , like the Household Support Fund , you do n't even need to be on benefits . Somne help comes from the Government-funded Households Support Fund scheme which is an initiative that gives local authorities money to spend on cost of living support in their area . The Labour Party government has expanded the scheme beyond 2024 . Ahead of Christmas , and with people focusing on the axed ? 300 Winter Fuel Allowance axe , people have been told to brush on whether they are eligible for the following payments . The sums - a whopping ? 4k - will help tide people over and afford @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ paid depends on what benefit you receive and the date you first received you benefit payment . Universal credit is paid monthly by the DWP . Attendance allowance , disability living allowance , pension credit , personal independence payment ( PIP ) and state pension are paid every four weeks after the date your received your first payment . Carer 's allowance , tax credits ( from HMRC ) and child benefit are either weekly or every four weeks . And maternity allowance is either every two weeks or every four weeks . If your benefit payment has n't arrived and you think you should have received it , double check the payment date on your awards notice in the first instance . If there has been a bank holiday or weekend , it may be delayed until the next working day . After that , contact your bank to ask if there is a payment waiting to go through . The payment might be pending and it might be a simple issue of the funds not having been cleared yet . Warm Home Discount - @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ one-off ? 150 discount off your electricity bill . If you 're eligible , your electricity supplier will apply the discount to your bill . The money is not paid to you . You 'll usually get the discount automatically if you 're eligible . You only need to apply if you 're on a low income in Scotland - contact your energy supplier to apply . You may be able to get the discount on your gas bill instead if your supplier provides you with both gas and electricity and you 're eligible . Contact your supplier to find out . You can still qualify for the discount if you use a pre-pay or pay-as-you-go electricity meter . Your electricity supplier can tell you how you 'll get the discount if you 're eligible , for example a voucher you can use to top up your meter . DWP Christmas bonus - ? 10 According to the UK government website : " The Christmas Bonus is a one-off tax-free ? 10 payment made before Christmas , paid to people who get certain benefits in the @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ of December . " A petition was launched in 2023 urging an immediate increase of more than ? 100 in the Christmas bonus , aligned with inflation . Almost a year later , the number of signatures on the petition is still growing as people call on Labour to act . At the time of writing , more than 15,000 people had thrown their support behind the petition , including 1,000 new signatories in a single day . " I 'm signing because so many older people are finding it hard " to make ends meet " during the cost of living crisis , " wrote Glenyd on the change.org page in support . " ? 10 is an insult , " added another supporter . Cold Weather Payment - ? 25 You may get Cold Weather Payments if you 're getting certain benefits or Support for Mortgage Interest . If you 're eligible , you 'll get these payments automatically . You do not need to apply , but you may need to tell Jobcentre Plus if you have a baby or if a @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ The payments do not affect your other benefits . You 'll get a payment if the average temperature in your area is recorded as , or forecast to be , zero degrees celsius or below over 7 consecutive days . You 'll get ? 25 for each 7 day period of very cold weather between 1 November 2024 and 31 March 2025 . State pension - ? 3,300 With 15 weeks left of the year , state pensioners are due for a maximum ? 3,300 before 2024 is out . The state pension is a payment made every four weeks by the government , to people who have reached the qualifying age and have paid enough National Insurance contributions . The Department for Work and Pensions ( DWP ) applies a formula , taking into account the number of full National Insurance years you have , contracted-out periods and the additional state pension you 've accrued , to work out have much state pension you 're due . If you 've never been contracted out , or earned any additional state pension , calculating how @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , say you have 25 qualifying years on your National Insurance record . For 2024-25 , you divide ? 221.20 by 35 and then multiply by 25 . Your new state pension will be about ? 158 a week . Winter Fuel Payment - ? 300 Chancellor Rachel Reeves has said that from autumn 2024 , anyone not on pension credit or other means-tested benefits will no longer get the winter fuel payment . Previously , everyone in England and Wales born before 25 September 1957 was entitled to the payment , which is worth either ? 200 or and ? 300 , depending on your circumstances . About 10 million people will be affected . While some say they do n't need the payment , a number of charities and MPs are concerned that people on a relatively small income will miss out . An estimated 880,000 eligible pensioners fail to claim pension credit . Household support fund - up to ? 600 The House Support Fund scheme was launched in October 2021 by the former Tory Government and has been extended ever @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ the Govenrment announcing announcing further funding of ? 421million to keep the scheme going until April 2025 . URL: http://extratime.ie/newsdesk/articles/16476/
COUNTRY: ie
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Hoops Worked Hard for Bray Win

CONTENT: On the May ... Holiday Friday night fixture in Tallaght Shamrock Rovers got back on track in the league by beating Bray Wanderers 2-0 and they maybe have the workers in the squad to thank for the victory . Let us be clear that it is not that any of the Rovers players have any issue with management . However the Hoops squad did get together themselves to talk about their responsibility for the recent run of form for Rovers that saw them come into the Bray game with three league defeats in the last four games . " We felt we needed to do it , " said Rovers striker Dean Clarke about the meeting the players had ahead of the Bray game . " We felt amongst ourselves that things were n't up to the standard of what we expect of ourselves . " It was a talk with a few of the experienced boys telling us what has to be done . We 've seen it straight away amongst ourselves in training and even tonight . We had more professionalism about us and hopefully that pays off . " We were a bit down , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ to Derry City ( 3-0 ) , home to Dundalk ( 2-0 ) and away to Cork City ( 2-0 ) last Monday . " We spoke about it amongst each other . We said ' Lads , we need to get our standards back up because for a couple of weeks they 've dropped ' . We have n't maybe played to our full potential at all this season . " We have enough experience here , with the likes of ( Stephen ) McPhail and Padge Cregg , ( Gary ) McCabe and Killian ( Brennan ) . They know themselves how to pick up a team and hopefully for the rest of the season we can kick on . " It was a timid enough opening half to the game but Rovers upped their game in the second half against a resolute Bray defence . The Hoops had known what to expect from the Seagulls and Dean Clarke himself had even attended their last league game so he could familiarise himself even further with last Friday 's opposition . Clarke was central to @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ was clear in on goal on 64 minutes when he was brought down by Bray 's Sean Harding on the edge of the box ; Harding received his marching orders for the foul from referee Tom ? s Connolly . While the incident gave the Hoops a man advantage , Clarke was more concerned that it prevented him getting on the scoresheet as he explained to extratime.ie after the game . " I was more disappointed that he did take me down to be honest as I wanted to get a goal . It was a big moment in the game . " " I actually watched them myself last week against St. Pat 's ( in a game Bray won 1-0 ) . I was quite impressed with them so I knew they 'd be compact in the first half so we just had to be patient . In the second half things opened up for the rest of the game . " The Rovers statistics ahead of the match had n't made pretty reading with Rovers coming into the game having failed to score in @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ . However , those stats also maybe helped the Hoops draw some inspiration . The pre-match stat pack had noted that Killian Brennan had n't scored in 21 games and Dean Clarke had n't scored in 10 games . After the red card foul , Killian Brennan lined up the resulting free kick to score and Clarke would soon get his first goal for Rovers since his move from Limerick in the close season . So extratime.ie asked the striker was he relieved to finally get his goal ? " It was a long time coming . I saw a stat that I still had n't got a goal in ten games so playing up front tonight I was really itching to get one . I 'm happy , I had a lot of family at the game so it was good that they were around . " Clarke rounded off a sweeping Shamrock Rovers move seven minutes from time involving substitute Danny North and right back Simon Madden . " It was one of Si 's trademark runs that you see him do 20 times a @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ lads , so I knew once I made that movement he would put the ball there . " You have to be patient and take your chances when they come so I 'm delighted that I did . We 've two games a week for the next two to three weeks so obviously it is the chance for me to keep in the team . " Pat Fenlon 's men take on Shelbourne in the quarter-final of the EA Sports Cup with an early 5pm kick off on Bank Holiday Monday due to floodlight issues at Tolka Park before they face Longford Town away next Saturday and Sligo Rovers in Tallaght on Tuesday week . " We were always under a bit of pressure because of the previous two results , " said Pat Fenlon speaking to Shamrock Rovers ' soundcloud account after the game . " The players showed good character to come back and win the game . The players know Monday ( against Cork City ) was n't good enough . " I do n't get too down about defeats or too up @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ corner so you have to focus on the next one . We 've picked up three points which is great . " Macdara made his debut as a football supporter in Lansdowne Road watching Ireland defeat Luxembourg . He still believes one day he will see his club Shamrock Rovers win an FAI Cup . You can follow Macdara on twitter @macdarabueller

URL: http://www.theguardian.com/world/2014/sep/25/woman-punched-california-officer-settlement
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Woman punched by California police officer to receive $1.5m ...

CONTENT: A woman punched repeatedly by a California highway patrol officer on the side of a freeway in an incident caught on video will receive $1.5m under a settlement reached on Wednesday , and the officer has agreed to resign . CHP commissioner Joe Farrow confirmed the settlement in an emailed statement and an attorney for Marlene Pinnock confirmed the dollar amount for the Associated Press . The agreement came after a nine-hour mediation session in Los Angeles . " When this incident occurred , I promised that I would look into it and vowed a swift resolution , " Farrow 's statement said . " Today , we have worked constructively to reach a settlement agreement that is satisfactory to all parties involved . " The statement said that Officer Daniel Andrew , who joined the CHP in 2012 and has been on paid administrative leave since the incident , " has elected to resign " . Andrew could still be charged criminally in the case . The CHP forwarded the results of its investigation of the incident to Los Angeles County prosecutors last month , saying he could face serious charges but none have been filed yet @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ form of a special needs trust for Pinnock , the CHP said . Pinnock 's attorney Caree Harper said the settlement fulfilled the two elements her side was looking for . " One of the things we wanted to make sure of was that she was provided for in a manner that accommodated her unique situation in life , " Harper said , " and that the officer was not going to be an officer anymore and we secured those things . " The July 1 video of Andrew punching Pinnock was captured by a passing driver and spread widely on the internet and television . According to a search warrant made public in court documents last month , Andrew had just pulled Pinnock from oncoming traffic and she resisted by pushing him after multiple drivers called 911 to report her walking barefoot along the side of the freeway . Andrew then straddled her on the ground as Pinnock resisted by " kicking her legs , grabbing the officer 's uniform and twisting her body " , the warrant said . Andrew " struck her @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ closed right fist " , the records say . The warrant said Pinnock suffered no signs of physical injury and refused medical treatment . She was placed on a psychiatric hold for two weeks . Pinnock has been diagnosed with bipolar disorder and had been off her medication for two to three months before the altercation . In an interview with the AP last month , Pinnock said she believed the officer was trying to kill her . " He grabbed me , he threw me down , he started beating me , " she said . " I felt like he was trying to kill me , beat me to death . "

URL: https://thetimes.co.uk/life-style/fashion/article/victoria-moss-size-16-fashion-front-row-g8p0ldpzg
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: How it feels to be size 16 on fashion's front row

CONTENT: When you attend a fashion show your name will often be written out in elaborate cursive on a small card to denote your place . Imagine these cards neatly lined along a narrow bench . There are about 3in , if you are lucky , on either side of the card . Usually , especially in Milan and Paris , they are comically close to each other . As someone whose bottom often takes up a couple of allocated spaces , my career as a fashion editor on the front row has always been a tight fit . Vanishingly few of my peers experience this . It is true that the fashion industry is fatphobic , but I had n't really thought this had affected my career much . Except perhaps when I look back and recall my Cyrano de Balenciaga era , when an editor made me ghost write first-person features to run alongside pictures of my thinner colleague proffering advice on how to get dressed . There was also the time , when applying for a job , that I was asked to send pictures of @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ samples of my work . I did n't get an interview . They hired a skinny blonde . Yet there have also been bonus episodes . I would have preferred to wear Primark than report on what it took to diet like a Victoria 's Secret Angel . Unlike Carrie Bradshaw , I would much rather eat dinner than buy Vogue . It , just , feeds me . Lately , among my industry colleagues , eating has gone the way of skinny jeans and power necklaces . Thanks to Ozempic , no one is really doing it any more . I go to fashion dinners ( thrown , say , to " celebrate " a new handbag coming out ) at exclusive restaurants . At one recently you could hear chairs scraping back in unison when dessert was popped down , guests moving themselves in case the cheesecake grew legs and leapt into their mouths . More transparent are the " dinnertoires " thrown for launches ( translation : no actual dinner ) . Increasingly worrying is the trend for ( mostly young ) slim women @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ minuscule catwalk samples they work with . There are others too who , after years of not being able to fit into the clothes they 've longed for , have relented , injected and found their newly snatched waists . I judge no one , but the masking effects for those with clear eating disorders is a huge concern . Advertisement There is a thread that connects the ultra-skinny frames on a catwalk with the bigger bodies that are so abhorred and derided . I was recently asked by friends why fashion has such a fetish for extreme skinniness . After I had given a token answer ( misogyny etc ) , the conversation shifted to seaweed crisps and plans to fit into party dresses . The cult of thin is so ingrained we do not even recognise it . Our refusal to accept ourselves as we are and our obsession with eulogising the perfect frame is never ending . Ashley Graham at Milan Fashion Week , 2023 GETTY IMAGES Injectable semaglutide might be the latest provocateur in the diet culture wars but the fashion @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ I worked on women 's magazines when casting a brunette was seen as diverse , when the singer Adele ( pre-makeover ) and the actor and director Lena Dunham might have been cover girls , but only from the neck up . I was used to stylists complaining when presented with a celebrity to dress beyond the sample size . " She was a chubby little troglodyte , " one fumed after returning from a shoot in Los Angeles where a Hollywood actress had the audacity to be too short and too fat for the clothes . When given compliments from these women , industrial amounts of salt would need to be swallowed . And yet fashion had its effect on me . At my slimmest I was a size 12-14 , achieved by a devotion to Marlboro Lights and vodka , with the odd salad and poached egg ( no toast ) . I might have wriggled into 29in waist jeans but my insides were rotten . I am now about a 16 , sometimes a little smaller , sometimes bigger , but on Wes Streeting 's hit @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ ago , exhausted by critiquing my body and finding endless fault , worrying about whether I should have milk in my coffee , panicking about pasta and living in fear of my knees , I ( and my therapist ) decided I could spend the rest of my life hating myself or I could just not . I can not recommend anything more . Now 43 , I have a fairly low interest in other people 's opinion of me , which helps , as did the idea that my daughter ( seven ) would pick up on any of this . She is thankfully unaware that one day someone will make a judgment about her based on her body . Advertisement Whatever your dress label , it is important to understand why clothes do n't fit . Spoiler : it 's nothing to do with your body . At college , fledgling designers are taught to create on a size 8 . One household name joked to me recently that " they do n't teach grading at fashion school " . This is the problem . It @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ a garment is sized up ( or down ) from the sample . It can be highly inaccurate . When badly executed , it is why you might try on a blazer ( as I did in John Lewis the other week ) to find it too small at the waist but enormous on the arms . It is no surprise that most of them are terrified of tits . I sometimes think the fashion industry 's opinion of bigger bodies lies not in its pursuit of itsy bitsy scraps of material , but the artful flex of the oversize trend . The very name is comical -- these clothes are so big ! But these clothes fit some people quite well . A residual effect is that bigger people can now buy into these brands , which are catering to them only by mistake . I have mixed feelings about this . There have been some skirmishes into casting curvier women on catwalks , but after a few seasons ' churn , fashion , as it has done this year , reverts to its preferred type . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Vogue , wrote an open letter asking for samples to be made bigger . Later that year , the designer Mark Fast caused a furore by daring to put size 12 women in bodycon knit dresses at his London Fashion Week show . The same conversations are still happening . The model Ashley Graham is more likely to be benched on the front row than strut on the catwalk . When the size 16 Paloma Elsesser won model of the year at the 2023 Fashion Awards , she received so much vitriol that she abandoned her Instagram account . Mark Fast 's London Fashion Week show , when he used size 12 models AFP When curvier models are used , they are either draped in a kaftan or half naked . It is sledgehammer inclusivity . Look ! We booked a bigger girl ! See every fleshy lump of her ! While it is tokenistic , it is also unhelpful . What about showing you can cut tailoring for a size 14 ? That most luxury conglomerates find themselves in a downturn is unsurprising in many @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ . But I wonder about the disconnect between the young , very skinny girls on the catwalk and their customers . Has this narrow representation of women finally turned us off ? Advertisement It is not all gloom . The years of churn have shifted the dial . There are brands and designers embracing the financial reality that catering to more women simply makes good business sense . Finding clothes in larger sizes that you want to wear is harder work , but the pay-off can be greater . Perhaps it makes your style more interesting or individual , rather than mindlessly pulling on the same thing as everyone else because Alexa Chung looked nice in it . I have never turned up to a wedding wearing the same dress as another guest . Take the wins , and here 's my guide on where to buy them . Where I buy my clothes Never buy anything just because it fits . Is it nice or just a weird muumuu that covers you up ? Good American is the only denim brand I will @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ jeans ( in grey , blue and white ) , which stop slightly above my shoe ( I am 5ft 6in ) . You do n't have to worry about trailing hems , and a flash of ankle can be helpful . Flaunt a sock . Selfridges has a big edit , but I order direct from the US website too ( worth keeping an eye on for its sales ) . Buy in duplicate ( see above ) . When you find something great , capitalise on it . Advertisement Experiment and ditch all the clich ? s about what you think you are supposed to wear . I never thought I 'd wear white jeans , but since trying them earlier this year I am obsessed . Ditto sleeveless knits , which I always thought were stupid until I tried one at a sale and now live in it . Universal Standard ( US sizes 00-40 ) is brilliant for everything from denim to workwear . I got a smart pair of black shorts here this summer . It ships from the US and @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ think outside the basic high street box . Size 12 people whinge about Zara coming up small . It 's not for us . Top , Aligne . Jeans , Good American . Necklace , Pond London ROBERT WILSON FOR THE TIMES MAGAZINE Unsurprisingly , the affordable brands that are winning at the moment cater more broadly . Hurrah for Aligne ( 4-22 ) -- the tailoring is bang on . Damson Madder ( 6-20 ) is strong for quirky frocks and leopard-print everything . Advertisement Connally Goods does great work making considered classics . It ships from Canada and is size infinite . You can order made-to-measure as well as off the rack ( XS to 6XL ) . The staff in the Ganni stores are incredibly helpful . They even bring extra sizes without having to ask . Runs to a 24 ( although not in everything and jeans only to a 32in waist , which is a bore ) . Tailoring is a bugbear . On the high street Marks & Spencer wins , but stretch to Autograph if you @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ a chic navy wool suit ( jacket and trousers ) available now that is very Kamala . I adore Molly Goddard ( 6-18 ) and Simone Rocha ( technically up to a 14 but her cut can be so big that I would say if you 're an 18/20 you could look here ) . Toogood is an acquired workhouse taste ( I love it ) , which runs unisex ( large ) sizing . Follow its Instagram account to get a heads-up on sample sales . It might be irritating , but lean into the oversized trend . I have a shirt from the Frankie Shop which my whole family could shelter under . Studio Nicholson is great for blazers and knitwear . Everyone raves about M&S cashmere but I find it comes up short and small . I prefer Uniqlo . Adjust your feed . I follow Aja Barber , who has an excellent Patreon newsletter that focuses on independent , sustainable brands ; Abisola Omole , who does a brilliant series of videos called " plus size passing " where she does try-ons @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ " normal " sizing ; Bethany Rutter , a colour magician full of helpful positivity , and Stephanie Yeboah , who has a really joyful way of putting clothes together . Vintage is not impossible , but because fashion has long excluded bigger sizes , it can be difficult to find things . In London keep an eye on Roomy ( @roomy.mkt ) , a plus-size secondhand pop-up market that has plans to extend around the country . Cutesy French brands ? Forget them all , apart from S ? zane , which runs to a 20 . Paloma Elsesser at Milan Fashion Week this year GETTY IMAGES I love a niche independent made to order . Stalf has great linen dresses and trousers . Omi Na Na is a trove of artisan designers that offer deliciously one-off pieces . Bourgine has cool separates and flouncy coats with a decent hit of Left Bank cool ( the store is in Paris but you can order made-to-measure online ) . I have not had success renting ( mostly because the pieces tend not to be @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ in extended sizing . Show your arms . And knees . The only person bothered about them is you . Without going too Trinny and Susannah , a scoop-neck top and dose of flesh is more flattering than covering up from neck to ankle . Trapeze-shaped tops are not your friend . Ditto Navajo poncho scarves , which have made an alarming return . Avoid . Ashley Graham on the Dolce & Gabbana runway last year GETTY IMAGES Layer knitwear to break up your dimensions a bit . A knitted vest over a T-shirt with a cardigan is more interesting than just a sweater .

TITLE: Dramatic moment Red Sox Hall-of-Famer Bill Lee COLLAPSES and is 'given CPR' during exhibition game live on ESPN... but 75-year-old is OK and 'feeling strong' after recovering to 'walk out of the sta

CONTENT: Dramatic moment Red Sox Hall-of-Famer Bill Lee COLLAPSES and is ' given CPR ' during exhibition game live on ESPN ... but 75-year-old is OK and ' feeling strong ' after recovering to ' walk out of the stadium ' Bill Lee collapsed during the Savannah Bananas ' game and was given CPR The former Red Sox pitcher was taken to a local hospital and is ' feeling strong ' The game was paused as Lee received medical attention , and then resumed Lee reportedly ' walked out of the stadium ' after the ' cardiac episode ' on Friday Former Boston Red Sox left-hander Bill Lee was said to be all right after collapsing on a bullpen mound and suffering a ' cardiac episode ' during an exhibition game on Friday in Savannah , Ga . Lee , 75 , got up and was assisted as he walked out of the stadium , according to Savannah 's WJCL News . Lee quickly received medical attention from firefighters , police officers and a paramedic , the Savannah Morning News said , before being taken to a local hospital . ' @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ not be alive right now , ' Town of Thunderbolt Administrator Bob Millie , also a firefighter who was in the right-field stands , said . ' He had a cardiac episode that stopped his breathing . That only ends one way normally , but their direct involvement saved his life . ' The owner of the team Lee was playing for , the Savannah Bananas , announced to the crowd , according to CBS Boston , ' He is back , he is OK , he is talking and he is feeling strong . Bill is gon na be all right . ' Red Sox Hall of Famer Bill Lee collapsed during an exhibition game for the Savannah Bananas Share this article Share 119 shares Millie said Lee was given CPR and an AED ( automated external defibrillator ) was used . The Bananas ' game against the Party Animals was paused briefly as Lee was tended to , then resumed . Lee starred for the Red Sox from 1969-78 , and the club inducted him into its Hall of @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ ( his only All-Star season ) , 1974 and 1975 , helping Boston reach the World Series in 1975 . Nicknamed ' The Spaceman ' for his colorful antics on the mound , Lee was a fan favorite in a career that concluded with four seasons with the Montreal Expos ( 1979-82 ) . In 14 major league years , Lee went 119-90 with a 3.62 ERA in 416 games ( 225 starts ) . TITLE: Nigel Slater's blackcurrant recipes

CONTENT: This column has ... been devoted to the pleasures of everyday cooking : a celebration of the simple , seasonal and satisfying , with barely so much as a nod to what you could call hobby cooking . So , nothing really for those who like to produce their own salami . But earlier this week I got a fancy to make baked Alaska , the simultaneously hot and cold dessert with layers of golden sponge and ice cream under a cloud of hot meringue that no one could exactly call " everyday " . It is a once-in-a-blue-moon way to end a meal for the both the eater and the cook , which is probably why I had so much fun making it . One can be too down to earth sometimes . If I 'm honest , I find the classic baked Alaska on the sweet side . With that in mind I have added a sharp sauce of blackcurrants to pour into the meringue as you shatter its sparkly crust with your spoon . Yes , such recipes use every damn bowl and pan in the kitchen and I had to rethink my usual rather laid-back speed of @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ your Alaska is not to end in sweet , creamy , tears . ) But the result is gorgeous and left us sinking our spoons through meringue into cold ice cream and soft sponge cake like seven-year-olds at a birthday party . Do n't even think of using shop-bought cake . The sort of cook who has a blow torch will find the final bit of the recipe a doddle . Those who have n't , like me , may have to keep their fingers crossed . Ice cream in the oven , what could possibly go wrong ? Blackcurrant compote blackcurrants 350gcaster sugar 80gwater 3 tbsp Remove the blackcurrants from their stalks , dropping them into a small saucepan . Add the sugar and water , then bring to the boil . Lower the heat , and let the currants simmer gently for 4 or 5 minutes , then remove from the heat as soon as the sugar has dissolved and the berries are starting to burst . Pour into a jug or bowl and set aside . A sense of urgency is required @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ baking tray or swiss roll tin approx 30 x 22cm , lined with baking parchment . Set the oven at 170C/gas mark 3-4 . Put the butter into the bowl of a food mixer , add the sugar and beat till light , creamy and soft . Occasionally scrape down the sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula to help the ingredients to combine smoothly . Break the eggs into a bowl , beat lightly with a fork to mix the yolks and whites then add to the butter and sugar a little at a time , the beater still turning , till the eggs are fully incorporated . Mix the flour and baking powder then fold into the batter . Transfer the mixture to the lined baking tray , smooth the surface then scatter the blackcurrants on top . Bake in the preheated oven for 35 minutes ( check after 30 ) . Remove from the oven , leave to cool then turn out on to a cooling rack and peel off the paper . When the sponge is cool enough , cut out @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ ) . Transfer the discs to a parchment-lined baking sheet , leaving a little room around each . Make 6 large balls of ice cream with a scoop , place them on a metal or plastic tray and return them to the freezer until hard . To make the meringue , put the sugar on a baking sheet and warm in a low oven for 5-7 minutes . Whisk the egg whites in a clean food mixer till thick and fluffy . With the whisk still turning , add the warmed sugar in 2 or 3 batches , then continue whisking for 4 or 5 minutes till firm and glossy . Fold in the cornflour . Set the oven at 220C/gas mark 7 . Press the centre of each sponge disc with the back of a spoon to create a hollow . Working quickly , place a ball of ice cream in the centre of each piece , then cover each with a thick layer of meringue , enclosing the entire cake , right down to the baking sheet . There should be no cake or ice @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ for 3-5 minutes till the meringue starts to colour . Alternatively , if you have a kitchen blowtorch , lightly brown each one all over . Lift on to cool plates and serve with the compote ( above ) . Put the water and sugar into a medium-sized saucepan and bring to the boil . Cut the peaches in half , remove the stones , then lower the fruit into the syrup . Reduce the heat and leave to simmer for 7-10 minutes or until the peaches are fully tender . Remove the peaches from the pan with a draining spoon and place them snugly , hollow side up , in a serving dish . Place a tablespoon of mascarpone in each peach half . Crumble the amaretti on top . Warm a little compote in a pan , then spoon over the peaches and serve .

CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Graphic Novel: Heart of Darkness

CONTENT: Graphic Novel : Heart of Darkness I 've always had trouble with Marlon Brando 's performance in Apocalypse Now ; his Kurtz is far from Conrad 's bed ridden , emaciated ivory dealer . This depiction offers a more than fitting visual interpretation of Kurtz , with his universe-encompassing stare depicted in a manner that live action will never manage . This is a hyper-reality that the illustrator Catherine Anyango capitalizes on , the paradox that sometimes illustration is the best way to depict real life . The literal animation of inanimate objects is rendered beautifully , the " carcass " of an old steam engine being a perfect example . The frequent depiction of events from a bird 's eye view give a sense of two-dimensional progression along a map and the landscapes seen from this perspective often take on the appearance of some great animal 's skin . The pareidolia found in the original work is also a strong feature of the graphic novel , so strong as to reverse itself at times : faces found in scenery turn into scenery found in faces . The entire novel is drawn in black and white , though a sort @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Persepolis . The illustration is done in pencil , though often crossing into inky territory , where bold smudges are reminiscent of cartographical inkblots or violent blood splatter . Intense charcoal portraiture and sketch is used alongside this ; the former being one of the novels most effective illustrative skills . It should n't escape our attention that the narrator , Marlowe , is given Conrad 's own countenance . In written style the graphic novel is just as brief as the book . In place of broad dialogues , individual lines are singled out and placed on a pedestal of dramatic image . Undoubtedly a vast part of Heart of Darkness ' literary beauty is forgone , but this is inevitable in such a densely poetic novel and can be forgiven . The lines that have been chosen remain powerful , particularly alongside highly charged pictures ; in this way , Conrad 's literary beauty is left intact and in charge .

TITLE: Norwich City announce price rise for 2022-23 season ...

CONTENT: Based on adult seats in the most expensive standard seating areas - the central seats in the South Stand and the City Stand , that are renewed before the first deadline - the rise equates to just over ? 1 per game in the Premier League or just under ? 1 in the Championship . While the cheapest season tickets rise by just over ? 1.80 in the top flight or just over ? 1.50 if the club are relegated , which would mean 23 home games rather than 19 . A statement announcing the pricing for the 2022-23 season , confirms : " The club 's popular interest-free direct debit scheme remains in place , allowing supporters to spread the cost of their season ticket over the course of the year . " Season tickets also have priority access to any home cup fixtures and selected club events held at Carrow Road over the course of the 2022-23 season , as well as 10 per cent discount on official club merchandise and exclusive Yellow Rewards from local and national businesses . " For the first time in 10 years and owing to the @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ pandemic , season ticketing pricing has been adjusted . The club has worked hard to ensure that this adjustment in pricing is minimal . " Norwich City season ticket prices for 2022-23- Credit : Norwich City FC Previously , season ticket prices rose by between 8pc and 10pc for the 2013-14 campaign , when the Canaries were becoming established in the Premier League . However , a price freeze came in when renewal packages were confirmed for the 2014-15 campaign - for those who renewed ahead of the first deadline - that has remained in place ever since . With City navigating a loss of expected income of around ? 30million due to the Covid-19 pandemic during recent years and with the UK at its highest rate of inflation for almost 30 years at 5.4pc , that has been unfrozen . Around 14,000 of almost 21,000 season ticket holders use the club 's direct debit payment scheme , which is collected on the last working day of each month . These will be automatically renewed . The pandemic meant that the vast majority of @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ clearer that was likely to continue , payments for the 2020-21 season were taken forward and used for season tickets for the current 2021-22 season . This meant there was no renewal process last year . It 's understood the decision to introduce the increase was signed off by the club 's board of directors after initial work by the commercial management team . TITLE: Etihad inks reciprocal interline deals with five airlines

CONTENT: Etihad inks reciprocal interline deals with five airlines Etihad Airways has launched reciprocal interline partnerships with five new airline partners , further expanding travel options for guests across its expanding global network . The airline has agreed partnerships with Kam Air in Afghanistan , SKY express in Greece , Rex Airlines in Australia , Jeju Air in Korea and Myanmar Airways International . The interline agreements give customers of all airlines involved enhanced connectivity to destinations across each other 's networks , enabling guests to book their entire journey on a single ticket and have their baggage checked through seamlessly to their end destination . At Abu Dhabi 's Zayed International Airport guests from all over the world can connect to the Kam Air flight to Kabul and travel onward into Afghanistan . Guests flying on Etihad 's twice daily flights to Athens , can connect onwards with SKY express to 28 destinations in Greece and its beautiful Mediterranean islands . Rex Airlines provides travellers with effortless access from Etihad 's gateways in Sydney and Melbourne to 22 destinations in Australia , including Adelaide , Hobart , Canberra , Brisbane , and @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ continue their journey from Seoul to 27 destinations across seven countries in Northeast Asia . Links with Myanmar Airways International will give enhanced access to Yangon and Mandalay through Etihad 's gateways in South Asia . Etihad now has 123 interline , codeshare and strategic partnerships with airlines across the world . TITLE: STAR TIME I was starstruck every day filming alongside Holding stars, says Clinton LibertyTHE Virgin Media series was first released on ITV and has been recently aired on the Irish broadcasting stat

CONTENT: Clinton went on to explain that he received a lot of advice from his fellow stars . He said : " They had stories upon stories of just of their time in the industry . " They had advice upon advice and I just learned so much from all of them . " They were so nice and so caring as well and they gave me a lot of advice and tips in terms of longevity in terms of my career and happiness and life outside of acting . " The Normal People star also admitted that he has received nothing but positive feedback from viewers . He explained : " It 's been absolutely amazing . The feedback has been really really good I could n't ask for better feedback . " And also one thing I really love about it all is when people tell me , ' Oh I 'm watching the show , ' it 's usually with their families so it 's not just them . FABULOUS FEEDBACK " It 's like , ' Oh I 'm watching the show but I 'm watching it @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ is obsessed with the show , ' or , ' My granny is watching the show as well ' . " It 's really good to know that you 're part of something that resonates with whole generations in one family . " I remember when I was in Westfield in London a few months ago when the show initially aired it was a little kid who recognised me and dragged her mam over and she was like , ' It 's him , it 's him ' . " I was like if the show resonates with the kid and it resonates with the mum it 's really good . " I 'm very proud to be part of something like that . " Clinton also revealed that he had n't read the book before he got the part and that it was different from what Graham Norton had initially written . He explained : " My character actually in the book he was significantly older than I was . " So when Kathy Burke our director saw me they basically just @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ the role to me . " So they changed a little bit because Linus was a young detective that had fast tracked his way through the ranks and it was really fun to do some investigation on like detective work . FAMILY AFFAIR " I had heard about the book but I never actually read it until I got the role and then I was like , ' Okay it 's time to really break down this character and break down the whole story ' . " The actor also revealed that he got his mother into the acting industry . He explained : " It was a film called Aisha directed by Frank Berry and starring Letitia Wright . " And yeah I remember the casting director Elaine Grainger was looking for like someone to play the mother of the protagonist which is played by Letitia Wright and I just suggested my mam . " I was like , ' What 's the worst that could happen ? ' And put my mam on tape and my mam was a natural and @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ off and she got another callback and then she got the role and I was like wow what happened . " She absolutely loved it . " For me , I think it was really good because I 'm always telling my mam about working on sets working with all these amazing people but for her to actually have that first hand and to see what it 's really like on set it 's so good .

URL: https://thetimes.co.uk/uk/science/article/how-good-is-your-greek-read-this-roman-scroll-and-win-400000-2hnhhkncx
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: How good is your Greek? Read this Roman scroll and win 400,000

CONTENT: Researchers in Oxford have deciphered part of a Roman papyrus scroll that was turned into a lump of carbon when Mount Vesuvius erupted nearly 2,000 years ago . The feat is a milestone in the effort to recover what may have been one of the finest libraries in antiquity . However , only a few Greek letters have been read so far and nearly ? 400,000 in prizes is on offer to anyone who can make further progress . The story of the Bodleian Library scroll , which is kept in the Bodleian Library in Oxford and known to scholars as PHerc.172 , began in Herculaneum , a town 12 miles northwest of Pompeii . Rsearchers have made use of the world 's most powerful x-ray machines , which are available to Oxford scholars GETTY In AD79 Vesuvius erupted , sending a surge of ultra-hot gas over the villa in which the papyrus was kept . As molten rock and pumice rained down , the scroll became a carbonised lump buried under ash that was metres deep . Advertisement It would be centuries before it again saw the light of day but in the early 1800s , PHerc.172 and five other carbonised scrolls were given by Ferdinand IV , the king of Naples and Sicily , to the future George IV of England . It is said they were exchanged for some kangaroos . The brittle charcoal-like scroll would crumble if anyone tried to unroll it but the material has been scanned by one of the world 's most powerful x-ray machines at the Diamond Light Source facility in Harwell , south of Oxford . This has given a 3D digital representation which has allowed the tightly wound individual layers of papyrus to be examined . Special software was used to virtually " unroll " the 3D model and this data has been made available to the public via the Vesuvius Challenge project , created by Nat Friedman , a Silicon Valley investor . Since spring 2023 , people have been invited to compete for cash prizes by helping to read other Herculaneum scrolls scanned in the same way . Advertisement The competitors ' tasks include developing software that can detect the ink , only some of which can be seen in the scans . This is a difficult because both the writing and the papyrus are carbon-based and black . PHerc.172 is the fifth roll from Herculaneum to be made part of the competition . This year three students shared a prize of $700,000 for using AI to read parts of another scroll which had been given to Napoleon by Ferdinand in 1802 and is now held by the Institut de France in Paris . That papyrus was revealed to be work by Philodemus , an Epicurean philosopher and poet . Only a portion of it has been read so far . The Bodleian Library is at the centre of research efforts ALAMY The Oxford papyrus has its own characteristics , the competition organisers said , and these could mean it is more legible . " This is the first time the ink of a Herculaneum scroll has presented with such clear contrast in x-ray CT , " they explained . This suggests that a higher density ink was used than in the Paris papyrus . In some parts , the writing is only clear every other line . " Did the scribe dip their pen once every two lines ? " they asked . Advertisement Papyrologists quickly confirmed that the text of the Oxford scroll is Greek . " We 're on the cusp of recovering complete words , " the competition organisers said . This includes what appears to be the start of the Greek word d ? at ? ? p ? , which would mean something like " confusion , agitation , or disgust " . The villa in which the scrolls were found was discovered in 1750 . It might have been the most splendid in Herculaneum and was possibly built by Julius Caesar 's father-in-law . What appears to be a Greek philosophy library , with about 800 scrolls , has been excavated but much of the structure is still to be explored . The building might also contain a larger Roman library . Historians speculate that such a room could hold lost histories , poetry and drama , the recovery of which would be comparable to the resurrection of classical culture seen during the Renaissance .

TITLE: Legan Sarsfields GAA to #Run4Pieta this weekend to support ...

CONTENT: Legan Sarsfields GAA to #Run4Pieta this weekend to support Pieta House Pieta House Darkness Into Light fundraiser not forgotten as local club plans 24 hour run Reporter : Jessica Thompson 8 May 2020 Email : **34;1272;TOOLONG 0 The annual Darkness Into Light walk , which sees thousands of people across the country get up in the wee hours of the morning and walk until the sun comes up , has had to be cancelled this year , due to Covid-19 . Unfortunately , that has meant a serious decrease in funding for the worthy charity , which offers so much to those engaging in self-harm , with suicidal ideation , or bereaved by suicide . The charity relies on the generosity of the public whose donations and fundraising make up over 80% of their income . But if there 's one thing the coronavirus has done right , it 's bringing the community together in support of others and that 's exactly what Legan Sarsfields GAA are doing this weekend to support Pieta House . GAA clubs across the country have been asked to help raise @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ not much GAA going on , clubs are jumping at the chance to provide for others . Legan Sarsfields GAA are taking on a 24 hour consecutive running fundraising challenge this weekend , which will involve running from 5.30am on Saturday , May 9 into the light of 5.30am on Sunday , May 10 . The club will be holding part of this at Whyte 's shop where there will be an opportunity for people to support the cause . All strict HSE guidelines will apply . URL: http://www.irishtimes.com/sport/rugby/international/michael-cheika-one-of-the-big-things-we-want-to-bring-is-honesty-1.2405618
COUNTRY: ie
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Michael Cheika: 'One of the big things we want to bring is honesty'

CONTENT: Michael Cheika : ' One of the big things we want to bring is honesty ' Battered Wallabies count injury cost of battle with Pumas ahead of final Australia head coach Michael Cheika congratulates David Pocock after the Rugby World Cup semi-final victory over Argentina at Twickenham . Photograph : Dan Mullan/Getty Images Not exactly La Bombonera but for this night Twickenham 's groove was nothing like a rugby stadium . The 80,025 crowd was riddled with passionate Argentineans and plenty of cranky , worried Aussies . There are moments in every game that can change what seems inevitable . When these moments came , Argentina were unable to nail down the tries required to continue rattling rugby 's world order . Michael Cheika 's structurally sound , experienced and well-prepared Wallabies did to them what they did onto Ireland a week beforehand . But what made this such a special happening was Los Pumas unbreakable spirit . It was finally fractured just enough by a wondrous Drew Mitchell run -- he being one of Cheika 's Giteau rule returnees -- which was finished off by Adam Ashley-Cooper completing a hat- trick of tries . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ former Leinster coach 's liking . " We let them back in it , " said Cheika . Australia 's start was perfect . At 1.09 on the clock Rob Simmons dived over the try line after intercepting Nicolas Sanchez 's reverse pass . Cathal Garvey from the UL Bohemians think-tank , now a Wallaby analyst , might get some credit for highlighting that early preference by the Argentine outhalf . Double bluff The second try , on nine minutes , was another of those double bluff attacks that have become Australia 's trademark at this tournament . Lull a studious defence into a sense of certainly about what you intend doing , then catch them cold with Bernard Foley 's cracking left-handed skip pass to Ashley-Cooper . It 's not up for debate that Tomas Lavanini did n't wrap his right arm when chopping Folau . " Yellow card offence , " was Barnes 's first idea . TMO Ben Skeen offered the Englishman an exit strategy : " Here 's it in real time , Wayne , and the wide angle @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ " Maybe give the player the benefit of the doubt . " Common sense was about to prevail until Barnes suddenly turned , marched over to Lavanini and blazed the yellow card to high heaven . " Tomas stretched his arms , there is a huge impact and he could n't close his arms , " said coach Daniel Hourcade . " We have to respect this decision . " This was when we saw the difference between Australia with and without Folau and David Pocock . The latter 's perfectly timed smash on Juan Mart ? n Hernandez after five minutes ended El Mago 's input by the 43rd . Scramble defence Some brave scramble defence somehow denied Folau a fine individual try but when power did n't pay they turned to the subtle brilliance of Matt Giteau . The veteran 's beautiful long pass saw Ashley-Cooper in for his second try . Sanchez 's five penalties cut into the the lead but the chance so magically created before half-time simply had to become seven points . Phenomenal foot work by little Santiago @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Hernandez 's one-handed fling went behind Joaquin Tuculet with the line at his mercy . No Creevy , Imhoff or Hernandez and seemingly no hope , yet Argentina remained loyal to their attacking philosophy that got them to Twickenham in the first place . Problem was Australian nous . The only team to beat New Zealand in 2015 have the ability to slow almost every attacking opportunity -- mainly because of Michael Hooper 's tentacles . But this remained a genuine battle befitting of a World Cup semi-final . That is until Mitchell 's run justified his journey from Toulon exile . A savvy media operator , Cheika easily deflected the great revival theory that was laid at his feet afterwards . " I do n't like going with those clich ? s , the winning team and all that . . . One of the big things we want to bring is honesty and let that be seen by the way we play the game . Not talk about doing certain things and try to market that or promote it . Just be honest , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ But this victory comes at a price . Ironically Pocock 's kamikaze tackle on Amorosino , while essential , as an offload meant an Argentina try under the posts , put Folau 's dodgy ankle in a new bind . Scott Fardy and Giteau also hobbled off , while Hooper and Pocock looked battered by the end . Just as Steve Hansen had hoped , Argentina did New Zealand an enormous favour . What 's left in the tank ? " Probably about 80 minutes , " went Cheika with that crazed yet controlled smile . TITLE: Dundee 0-1 Celtic: Rodgers happy with adaptable side

CONTENT: " Very satisfying ... " said Rodgers . " These are the types of games that you need to see through . " We only needed the one goal and , after that , we defended very , very well . Great victory for us . " Celtic had drawn 3-3 with Manchester City in Wednesday 's enthralling Champions League encounter in Glasgow . " Naturally , there was a little bit of sharpness missing from our game today with so much over the last number of weeks , in particular the game during the week , " Rodgers explained . " The important thing today was the result . The last week has shown us what a really , really good team we are . Paul Hartley was pleased with Dundee 's performance " Over the course of the week , we 've shown that we can be really offensive - on our last game against Kilmarnock , 6-1 , the midweek game was outstanding at the really top level of football - and we showed another side to come away from home and see out a 1-0 win . " Rodgers also praised match-winner @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ place ahead of Dorus de Vries , who had displaced the Scot earlier in the season . " Again , brilliant today , " Rodgers said of Brown . " Really dominated the middle of the field . He 's a fantastic player . From the experience I have working with really outstanding midfield players , he 's right up there . A great goal . " I thought Craig Gordon was brilliant . Hopefully , you see the improvement in terms of his distribution . The build-up part of our game is vital to how we play . " Dundee boss Paul Hartley was not too discouraged , despite his side sitting on six points from eight games going into the international break . " We were in the game all the time , " explained . " The players were terrific . We were brave . " Celtic did have a lot of possession , but they never really cut us open . " Onwards and upwards for us .
